text: The United States and the United Kingdom announced new sanctions against Houthi Rebels today following a series of attacks on commercial shipping vessels in the Red Sea.

Global shipping giant Maersk has officially announced it will reroute all vessels away from the Red Sea, opting instead for the longer journey around the Cape of Good Hope due to severe security risks.
Egypt reported a sharp 40% decline in Suez Canal transit revenues this month, citing the ongoing Red Sea shipping attacks as the primary cause for the drop in traffic.

The United Nations expressed deep concern over the escalating Red Sea crisis, warning that the disruption to global supply chains will lead to increased inflation affecting nations in Europe and Africa.

In response to the Houthi Rebels' actions, the European Union launched Operation Aspides, deploying naval assets to the Red Sea to protect commercial shipping and secure the critical maritime supply chain.
